EDF-2026-DA-ACC-AIRDEF-EATMI.title: High-end endo-atmospheric interception
EDF-2026-DA-ACC-AIRDEF-EATMI.description:
Обхват:

Тази тема има за цел да проследи и допълни предишни дейности за разработването на ендоатмосферен прихващач чрез разработване на ключови технологии, необходими за успешно противодействие на хиперзвукови и балистични заплахи до ниво на технологична готовност 6. Тя следва да се основава на конкретни концептуални проекти и да усъвършенства необходимите технологии чрез разработване на специални демонстрационни системи, например за корпуса на въздухоплавателното средство, задвижването, разделянето на платформите, смъртността, проследяването и придобиването на заплахи.

Тази тема допринася за целите на STEP, определени в Регламента за STEP, в целевата инвестиционна област на отбранителните технологии.

Режим на управление: Действие, изпълнявано при непряко управление от Организацията за сътрудничество в областта на въоръженията.

Форма на финансиране: Споразумение за финансов принос.

Възложени задачи:

Усвояване на безвъзмездни средства за действителни разходи след покана за участие в състезателна процедура.

Целеви видове дейности: Проучвания, проектиране, създаване на прототипи и изпитване на системи, без да се изключват дейности нагоре и надолу по веригата, допустими за действия за разработване.

Ориентировъчен бюджет: 100 000 000 EUR за тази тема в рамките на поканата за представяне на предложения по ЕФО-2026-ДА и 2 000 000 EUR за възнаграждението на упълномощения субект.

Ориентировъчен брой на предложенията, които ще бъдат финансирани: Едно предложение ще бъде финансирано по тази тема.

EDF-2026-DA-AIR-SPS.title: Self-protection systems
EDF-2026-DA-AIR-SPS.description:
Scope:

The aim of this topic is to follow up and complement previous activities in the field of the next generation self-protection systems in order to increase the survivability of fixed wing and rotary wing, combat or non-combat aircraft in hostile environments.

This topic is aimed at modular integration with both fixed and rotary wing aircraft, providing protection against direct and indirect threats based on open architecture and international standards, while protecting sensitive data and ensuring compatibility with other platforms. Key features include automatic coordination of countermeasures, threat detection and classification, and the ability to operate in complex environments. For indirect threats, the system should gather intelligence, disrupt enemy tracking and enable collaborative protection with other platforms.

This topic contributes to the STEP objectives as defined in the STEP Regulation in the target investment area of defence technologies.

Targeted types of activities: Design, system prototyping, testing and qualification, not excluding upstream and downstream activities eligible for development actions.

Indicative budget: EUR 24 000 000 for this topic under the EDF-2026-DA call.

Indicative number of proposals to be funded: One proposal is to be funded for this topic. However, depending on the quality of the proposals submitted and the budget available, more than one proposal may ultimately be funded for this topic.

EDF-2026-DA-AIR-STFS.title: Smart technologies for next generation fighter systems
EDF-2026-DA-AIR-STFS.description:
Scope:

The objective of this topic is to follow up and complement previous activities in the field of smart technologies for next generation fighter systems. It aims to explore future architectures and develop key technologies, including with proofs of concept, demonstrations and prototypes, for the next generation of military integrated modular avionics for use on a variety of military aerial platforms, both manned and unmanned aircraft, in the context of a defence air cloud.

This topic contributes to the STEP objectives as defined in the STEP Regulation in the target investment area of defence technologies.

Targeted types of activities: Integrating knowledge, studies, design and system prototyping, not excluding upstream and downstream activities eligible for development actions.

Indicative budget: EUR 25 000 000 for this topic under the EDF-2026-DA call.

Indicative number of proposals to be funded: One proposal is to be funded for this topic. However, depending on the quality of the proposals submitted and the budget available, more than one proposal may ultimately be funded for this topic.

EDF-2026-DA-ENERENV-HPES-STEP.title: High-performance energy systems
EDF-2026-DA-ENERENV-HPES-STEP.description:
Scope:

This topic aims to follow up and complement previous activities in the field of high- performance energy systems, with a particular focus on unexplored technologies such as hydrogen (conversion, storage and logistics). New use cases for stationary military applications will therefore be identified and defined, while innovative building blocks and functional modules will be developed and evaluated. The demonstrators’ power and energy targets and other key performance indicators, including life cycle considerations, are expected to be improved, while the operability and connectivity to the energy grid systems of operational bases will be improved.

In addition, this topic aims to advance the creation, revision or consolidation of military standards for products and processes for energy generation, distribution and storage.

Beyond defence technologies, this topic also contributes to the STEP objectives as defined in the STEP Regulation in the target investment area of clean and resource efficient technologies.

Targeted types of activities: Studies, design, system prototyping and testing, not excluding other activities eligible for development actions.

Indicative budget: EUR 20 000 000 for this topic under the EDF-2026-DA call.

Indicative number of proposals to be funded: One proposal is to be funded for this topic. However, depending on the quality of the proposals submitted and the budget available, more than one proposal may ultimately be funded for this topic.

projects.title: European Next Generation Rotorcraft Technologies Phase II
projects.objective:
The ENGRT II (EU Next Generation Rotorcraft Technologies) project is a pivotal initiative designed to bolster the European Union's strategic autonomy and ensure its leadership in defense capabilities. By preparing the technological foundations for the next generation of European military rotorcraft, ENGRT II directly contributes to the security of EU citizens and enhances the global competitiveness of the European Defence Technological and Industrial Base (EDITB).
The project aims to deliver cutting-edge rotorcraft technologies ready for integration by 2030, addressing the complex and evolving demands of modern defense.
Building on the successes of its predecessor, ENGRT I, this project pushes the boundaries of innovation across several key domains. It focuses on developing advanced rotorcraft configurations, novel materials, and breakthroughs in aerodynamics to ensure future platforms outperform existing models in speed, range, and operational capability.
A core objective of ENGRT II is to unify the European rotorcraft community around critical strategic topics. The project will establish a common European perspective on a Modular Open System Approach (MOSA), enabling greater interoperability and adaptability for future systems. Furthermore, it will develop key technologies for rotorcraft operating in Multi-Domain Collaborative Combat, a crucial element of modern military doctrine.

 

By fostering intensive cross-border cooperation between industry leaders, research institutions, and 10 specialized SMEs from across the Union, ENGRT II strengthens the EDITB's resilience. This collaborative framework not only reduces reliance on non-EU technology sources but also promotes a more integrated and innovative European defense ecosystem.

The project also emphasizes life-cycle efficiency, integrating sustainable practices to reduce the environmental footprint of rotorcraft operations while maximizing their effectiveness and affordability.

projects.title: Artificial Intelligence Warfare Adaptive Swarm Platform
projects.objective: The AI-WASP “Artificial Intelligence Warfare Adaptive Swarm Platform” project aims to develop a pioneering solution of AI-based multifunctional aperture and transceiver. The project targets to develop multifunctional solutions for electromagnetic spectrum operations in future electronic warfare battlefield along with missions for surveillance and establishing tactical mission network. The proposed solution is based on convergence of several existing technologies to a cost-effective multifunctional system, which is enabled by proposed modular open system architecture.  The multifunctionality is accomplished by developing completely new and novel system modules and software components utilizing Artificial Intelligence (AI) throughout the system. AI-WASP project develops a novel modular architecture, with hardware and software reusable modules, where separately developed technologies and functionalities are combined to one system that is designed to work both as an independent system and as networked and multitasking system of systems. The outcome of the project is a prototype of two different system variants that are to be integrated to small and medium size UAS and ground vehicles or ground station. The AI-WASP Consortium is coordinated by Patria and the consortium consists of 16 partners (LEs, SMEs, Mid-Cap and RTO) from nine EU Member States: Finland, Sweden, Estonia, Lithuania, Germany, Greece, France, Spain and Italy.

projects.title: 5G Communications for Peacekeeping and Defence 2.0
projects.objective:
5G Communication for Peace Keeping and Defence 2.0 will continue the consolidation and expansion of trusted EU Ecosystem standard for military multi-dimensional operations world-wide using 3GPP evolution. Overall objective is to enabling EDF Member States to procure and evolve coalition capability with complementary civil communication networks and devices. This should increase the European Defence Industry Technology Base, EU independence and security of supply.
The 5G COMPAD project overall reference architecture and use case definition will serve as a foundation of 5G COMPAD 2.0. The 5G COMPAD 2.0 project will bring the following on top of 5G COMPAD:
•	Advanced Capabilities such as network as a sensor, advanced positioning services, automatic network setup, non-terrestrial networks (NTN) and spectrum sharing
•	Enhanced individual systems and integration for full interoperability in a Multi-Domain Operational environment
•	Better Interoperability and integration with existing military infrastructure (existing NATO systems)
•	Standardized security solutions
•	Case-agnostic Minimum Viable Solution for Member States procurement of interoperable, secure, robust and resilient 5G for military use with TRL 7

Six Streams and associated tasks have been defined to address the requirements - Mandatory Studies (MS), Operational Requirements (OR)and Technical Requirements (TR) raised by the different MoDs. 
The capabilities will be demonstrated in several EU member states as well as during CWIX. Six demo events have been planned along the 3-year span of the project. 
The 5G COMPAD 2.0 project consortium consists of leading European 5G technology providers, 5G device providers, defence integrators, specialized SMEs, network operators as well as academia and research institutions.

projects.title: SEP-211097550 - Small UAS
projects.objective:
The consortium members would like to make a proposal to this call developing The Small Enhanced European UAS project as an advanced AI-driven swarm system consisting of 10 unmanned aerial systems (UAS), specifically designed for multi-domain operations in air, land, and maritime environments. The project addresses critical operational challenges faced by small UAS, including low signatures, extended operational ranges, autonomy, miniaturization, and survivability in contested and congested airspace.
Swarm Composition:
10 UAS with specialized capabilities:
2 UAS equipped with RF analyzers and jammers for electronic warfare.
2 UAS equipped with miniaturized weapons for precision targeting.
6 UAS with a variety of sensors, day and thermal cameras, and satellite communication systems for intelligence gathering and multi-sensor surveillance.
Payload and Propulsion Capabilities:
Each UAS is designed with a 20 kg payload capacity, allowing the integration of miniaturized weapons, sensors, jammers, and communication systems.
The system uses a combination of hydrogen propulsion for two UAS, ensuring long endurance and environmental sustainability, and hybrid engines with advanced batteries for the remaining eight UAS, optimizing performance and reducing acoustic signatures for stealth operations.
eVTOL (Electric Vertical Take-off and Landing) capability allows for versatile deployment and minimizes logistical constraints, while the detachable wings reduce the UAS's logistical footprint.
The swarm system will be controlled via an integrated C2 center with multi-level access, providing secure communication from a NATO Command Center (with 5 levels of access) directly to the swarm. The system features real-time operational control and the ability for direct interaction between the NATO Command and UAS units.
Quantum encryption will ensure that all communication between command centers, C2 units, and the UAS swarm is secured against cyber threats.

projects.title: Vertical Autonomous Next-generation Tactical Aerial European drone
projects.objective:
Drone operations have added a new chapter to modern warfare. Uncrewed systems are disrupting the way modern warfare is being fought, and rapid technological adaptation and continuous innovation in UAS will be critical in future warfare, therefore, also for the European security interests.

Therefore, VANTAGE project aims to develop the next generation of European tactical drones, improving operational efficiency and integrating cutting-edge C4ISR systems. The project’s ambition is to address key operational challenges such as limited operational range, vulnerability to detection, and the need for real-time data sharing. 

The project will develop a flexible and modular long-range VTOL platform that can perform various including C4ISR and kinetic strikes. This platform will be designed for adaptability, making it easy to integrate new sensors and navigation systems. This ensures that the drone can operate effectively in diverse environments, even in conditions where GPS/GNSS signals are denied or unavailable.

Considering the European needs, Ukrainian example and current technological state-of-the-art in the drone industry, VANTAGE expects to develop several disruptive and innovative technologies that will significantly enhance UAS performance in complex and contested environments: (1) GNSS-Denied and Electronic Warfare resilient Navigation; (2) Swarm and Manned-Unmanned Teaming; (3) Autonomous Flight Control and Dynamic Re-Planning and Integrated Multi-Operator Control; (4) 5G datalink.

Project consortium consists of 8 different entities from 6 EU countries. VANTAGE will increase cross-border partnerships within EU, involving SMEs, RTOs and Large Enterprises, for combining cutting-edge expertise and innovation capabilities.

projects.title: Cyber Infrastructure for Training in Advanced Defence Exercises and Learning
projects.objective:
CITADEL Range seeks to upgrade European defence technology in the field of cyber ranges. The vision is built on the strategic shift in the EU’s cyber defence agenda where a clear move has taken place from cyber responsive operations to full spectrum cyber defence operations. Given this, the next-generation cyber range modular products and concepts aim to provide tailored solutions for the entire value chain of cyber forces. This means that the focus is set strongly on 1) the needs of the EU and its Member States, and 2) the needs of holistic cyber defence as opposed to civilian cyber security. 

The project delivers a selection of advanced capabilities that enable cyber forces to conduct training, exercising, and mission rehearsal in realistic and cost-efficient environments purpose-built for cyber operations - be it national exercises for civil-military cooperation, multi-national exercises for rehearsing common mission execution or EU-wide training events coordinated by the EDA that enhance interoperability and promote seamless information-sharing within the Union. The project shall not deliver a new or standalone cyber range, it delivers technological tools and frameworks that complement already existing cyber ranges in national defence structures. The solutions are modular and thereby any individual result of the project can be used independently of others.

The solutions cover aspects such as cross-border classified exercises; realistic cyber operations toolkits and processes; and integration of cyber within MDO exercises. To better deliver these benefits, the project leverages technologies such as digital twins and AI that enable to create more realistic environments with less resources (time, money, people). All of this is built on top of a common domain-specific language and supported by EU-wide cyber range capability and trainee accreditation frameworks.

projects.title: Sustainable Energy Capabilities for Enhanced Military Camps and Operations
projects.objective:
SENTINEL addresses the urgent need to shift military energy provision from a high reliance on fossil fuels toward sustainable, resilient, and efficient energy solutions. The project aims to enhance the operational efficiency and tactical autonomy of military camps and operations, while reducing logistic burden and supporting a flexible and high-capacity energy supply. SENTINEL will prototype and qualify in operational conditions with armed forces 17 Key Energy Capabilities encompassing advanced energy technologies, enhanced mobility, digital energy solutions, and smart-grid integration, all tested in realistic military scenarios across various climates.

These capabilities will be demonstrated in five scenarios: (1) autonomous renewable energy systems in temperate and tropical tactical bases, (2) lifecycle demonstration of a hybrid energy base setup, (3) microgrid supporting advanced energy-efficient equipment in arid zones, (4) renewable energy-based operations in temperate and arctic conditions with hybrid and e-vehicles, and (5) a digital platform for designing and operating autonomous energy-efficient camps globally  based on digital twin components, applied for forward operating base of 2000 personnels to tactical operation of 50 personnels.

Built upon the EDF 2021 INDY project, SENTINEL brings together 41 entities, leveraging military and civilian expertise to deliver interoperable, secure, and sustainable energy solutions aligned with all stages of military operations. Key outcomes include enhanced resilience for high and low-intensity scenarios, reduced reliance on fossil fuels, and minimized logistic demands. Additionally, SENTINEL will strengthen the EU’s Defence Technological and Industrial Base by accelerating the military’s green and digital energy transformation, adapting civilian energy technologies for military use, and fostering continuous innovation for Key Energy Capabilities deployment between 2030 and 2050.

projects.title: ArmoURed Infantry Ground Assault
projects.objective:
The European Union (EU) aims to achieve greater strategic and industrial autonomy in defence and security sector. The obsolescence and the lack of interoperability of Armoured Infantry Fighting Vehicles (AIFVs) fleets is a significant concern for EU. Under this perspective, the AIFVs fleets are crucial to develop EU defence capabilities and promote military cooperation. Current conflicts have highlighted several AIFVs critical requirements such as protection, mobility, firepower and C4I / Situational Awareness as well as the need for standardization. AURIGA “ArmoURed Infantry Ground Assault” vision is to pave the way to respond to these needs.
AURIGA aims to design, develop and prototype key technology bricks, enabled by an open, scalable and modular system architecture that addresses both current and future threats and requirements. AURIGA system will incorporate innovative and disruptive technologies, and demonstrate the potential for upgrading existing AIFVs where applicable. AURIGA, will be aligned with the Concept of Operations (CONOPS) harmonized across participating EU Member States and Norway.
AURIGA modular and scalable design will offer a strategic and technological leap forward in the AIFV sector, addressing the limitations of ageing and obsolete systems by providing superior protection, enhanced firepower, advanced networking capabilities, increased mobility, future-proof adaptability and interoperability to excel in both conventional and asymmetric warfare while ensuring operational effectiveness and mission success. 
AURIGA will combine cutting-edge performance with cost-effectiveness, achieved through modularity and insertion of civil technology. The technology bricks examined under AURIGA will also pave the way for new applications and spin-offs in both defence and civil sectors.
AURIGA will be implemented through study and design, supported by prototyping, to identify capabilities and technologies in order to pursue project major objectives.

projects.title: Advanced Knowledge for indirect Trajectory Attack at long Range for EU states
projects.objective:
The AktarEUs project addresses the need for greater European defence autonomy and superiority by proposing further enhancements on the Beyond Line Of Sight (BLOS) technology.

Key objectives of AktarEUs are to: (1) Develop an integrated BLOS system in conditions representative of an operational use; (2) Progress in the development of EMS functions; (3) Integrate BLOS functionalities in a battlefield training system; (4) Foster cross-border cooperation, involving 4 SMEs and 1 Mid-Cap; (5) Enhance cost-effectiveness and lifecycle efficiency compared to existing BLOS solutions; (6) Further contribute to the integration of the European defence market with the involvement of 10 entities and support from the PESCO BLOS composed of 4 member states.

AktarEUs will be implemented through twelve work packages.

The project will begin with a feasibility study that will identify the main architectures constraints and mitigate most of the technological risks, followed by a design phase that will allow the creation of 3 main demonstrators (light, medium and heavy) which will be used in a final demonstration including live munition firings.

AktarEUs will be efficiently managed thanks to a concentrated consortium consistent with the development of demonstrator at a scale higher than the previous BLOS projects.

The consortium aims to achieve project objectives within 36 months, with an EDF support of 24,1M€. AktarEUs innovative approach and European collaboration will pave the way to the integration of BLOS systems within operational platforms in the early 2030s.

projects.title: Integrated Modular Unmanned Ground System 2
projects.objective:
iMUGS2 aims to advance the innovative technologies developed under the previous iMUGS project, focusing on unmanned ground systems (UGS), with higher levels of autonomy and interoperability for European defence forces.
The iMUGS2 project aims to develop an unmanned ground system capable of manned-unmanned and unmanned-unmanned teaming with other robotic unmanned platforms and manned infantry vehicles to provide them full battlefield support. The proposed solution will feature a modular, multi-agent system architecture, incorporating autonomy and swarming technologies, among others. 
The iMUGS2 solution aims to be free from any non-EU export control restrictions, balances performance and cost-effectiveness, reducing ownership costs through high maintainability and improved life cycle efficiency, while offering flexible, scalable procurement options that accelerate and future-proof capability acquisitions for European armed forces.
iMUGS2 will create an open ecosystem and large-scale European collaboration that will pave the way to enhanced unmanned capabilities ready for procurement and integration into EU forces by 2030. This will increase the competitive advantage of European companies in the worldwide UGS industry, fostering economic growth and high-tech job creation.

EDF-2024-DA-NAVAL-FNP.title: Functional smart system-of-systems under an integral survivability approach  for future naval platforms

projects.title:
Naval systEm of systems and 
integRatEd sUrvivability of future EU platformS
projects.objective:
NEREUS aims to develop the foundation of the Naval Smart System of Systems (SoS) for future European naval platforms, which should be able to operate interconnected in a fully integrated way under challenging multi-domain (i.e., land, aerial, surface, subsurface, and cyber) threat conditions and under an integral survivability approach.
The main objective of NEREUS is to perform the Conceptual Design of the System of Systems (SoS) for the next generation of European naval platforms. It will identify and define the common operational requirements of the SoS framework and the essential elements, structured in four pillars Combat System, Platform Management System, Communication & Information System and Navigation System, along with a Survivability Advisory System Foundation and Transversal capabilities.
NEREUS proposes a new architecture approach based on a SoS framework using a Service-Oriented-Architecture (SOA). The SoS Framework will integrate the essential elements of the ship in a coherent manner using a common infrastructure, including data and services, and promoting standardization, interoperability and interchangeability. This approach facilitates close coordination among all onboard systems to improve decision-making during operations, thereby maximizing survivability and optimizing the sustainment of Combat System capabilities according to the ship's conditions.
NEREUS will identify the elements to be incorporated into future European naval platforms, considering both current and emerging technologies. A comprehensive roadmap will be developed, identifying technologies and associated elements to be considered and developed for the future European naval platforms, and prioritising these developments for subsequent phases.
NEREUS is a 3.5-year project with a balanced consortium of 24 beneficiaries from 14 European countries, 12 large companies, 4 SMEs, 5 mid-caps, 2 research organisations, 1 naval industrial association,12 affiliated and 25 subcontractors.

EDF-2024-DA-SIMTRAIN-STME-STEP.title: Simulation and training for medical emergencies

projects.title: Technology-Enhanced Military Medical Training for Increased Readiness and Survivability in the Battlefield
projects.objective:
READYMED EUROPE aims to co-create, through a user-centred iterative methodology, a ground-breaking Military Medical Training Federated Platform that will significantly enhance both the preparedness and interoperability among military medical teams, while addressing challenges such as limited access, resource fragmentation, and outdated training methods. It will enable cost-effective and realistic training to improve decision-making, teamwork, and procedural skills critical in high-stakes environments including those involving mass casualties. READYMED seeks to transform military medical training, enhancing the EU's capacity to address critical defence challenges and ensuring the inclusion of personnel in remote locations.

The project will build on the consortium members’ state-of-the-art products for military medical training and the most advanced research outcomes from their major European EDF, EDIDP and Horizon projects in this domain. It will extend and integrate them using state-of-the-art simulation, extended reality, artificial intelligence, and 5G networking technologies to deliver a comprehensive and modular set of medical training modules for Prolonged Field Care, Tactical Combat Casualty Care, CBRNE Training, MASCAL and MEDEVAC Management, as well as Surgical Simulation. An advanced Exercise Control System alongside an AI-driven Learning Management System will the fine grain animation and analysis of joint exercises promoting interoperability in military and civilian medical training.

To lay the groundwork for broad adoption and successful exploitation of the project’s results, the consortium will produce an updated curriculum for prolonged field care that integrates the proposed innovations, develop a final report outlining best practices, recommendations, and a future roadmap for military medical training, and leverage its strong presence in the military medical training and civil protection markets to foster adoption.

projects.title: European Protected Waveform (Phase 2)
projects.objective:
The European Protected Waveform (EPW) program is a critical enabler for CSDP operations and missions by both EU member states and EU agencies in providing scalable, secure and resilient communications in peacetime and during operations with protection against intrusion, hacking, jamming, traffic monitoring and eavesdropping. The EPW system will facilitate European autonomy and secure cooperation between EU member states through full interoperability in complex and multi-domain operations over satellite. Synergies between EPW and existing or upcoming EU programs will be maximized to increase the efficiency and cost effectiveness of the EPW program and to integrate EPW within the future EU secure satellite communication ambitions.

The main objective of the multi-year project is to transform the system concepts developed in the first phase into an EPW-capable end-to-end system. The starting points for the second phase are three development tracks identified in the initial work of the EPW program. These focus on advanced satellite communication technologies for point-to-point as well as shared access communications, ensuring compliance with the mandatory system prototype and testing activities. Additional goals include the development of advanced technologies for resilience and end-to-end security.

The EPW consortium brings together a mix of organizations (SMEs, research centers, and large corporations) from across the EU that are experts in the field of satellite communications.

projects.title: European Autonomous Heavy Minesweeping System
projects.objective:
The EU critically depends on keeping shipping lanes open, both for international trade and for military support from allies. Sea mines are cost-effective weapons that can block these shipping lanes. Today, the EU lacks a minesweeping capability to counter mines that are a threat to large commercial ships. EUROSWEEP (European Autonomous Heavy Minesweeping System) will address this shortfall through the study, design, prototyping and demonstration of a common European unmanned minesweeping system with autonomous features that can generate the output required to protect large commercial ships and military re-supply vessels.
The project’s objectives are to: 
•	Give the EU a complementary mine countermeasures capability to be able to operate in different environments and be effective against more threats, such as modern stealth mines
•	Develop a concept of operations and collect Member States' (MS) requirements, to facilitate interoperability
•	Increase seaworthiness, endurance and shock resistance of maritime drones so they can operate longer and further while decreasing their underwater signatures  
•	Push the level of autonomy for maritime drones by integrating onboard sensors and data to plan, execute and adjust military missions without human assistance, understand the environment and operate in radio and/or GNSS-denied situations
•	Develop payloads for alternative missions such as anti-submarine warfare
•	Develop a cybersecurity framework to protect all digital infrastructure, data, information and communication against cyber attacks
•	Foster cross-border cooperation with a consortium comprising 18 partners from 10 MS and Norway, including 10 SMEs
With a total budget of 37M€, implemented through 12 WPs and achieving TRL6, EUROSWEEP will pave the way to the Future European Autonomous Heavy Minesweeping System, supporting EU independence from third countries for critical defence technologies while increasing EU innovation capacity, collaboration and competitiveness.

projects.title: Prediction and Response of Effectors on Critical Infrastructure and Structural Environments
projects.objective:
Current conflicts, such as those in Ukraine, the Middle East and the South China Sea, highlight the urgent requirement for advanced modelling tools to evaluate the effects of military operations on civilian infrastructure. To address these challenges, PRECISE offers three key features:
1.	High-resolution imagery collected from satellites and sensors (IR/EO, SAR and LIDAR) is annotated.
2.	AI-driven algorithms extract detailed information about structures, identifying vulnerabilities and patterns.
3.	PRECISE simulates various scenarios, including the impact of munitions on structures. This helps military planners to predict damage and make informed decisions to minimise collateral damage.

In line with the EDF-2024-LS-RA-CHALLENGE-SPACE-MSIAO, PRECISE incorporates four iterations to enable ongoing refinement. The project integrates outputs from the SPACE-MSIAO Challenge to improve data quality, modelling accuracy and predictive capabilities.
Furthermore, PRECISE contributes to the PESCO AMIDA-UT (Automated Modelling, Identification and Damage Assessment of Urban Terrain) initiative by providing tools for urban terrain analysis and damage prediction to enhance decision-making.

Thanks to nine partners and two affiliate entities from five EU member states: Belgium, Greece, France, Italy and Spain. The consortium is led by a mid-cap company (GMV) and comprises two large companies (INDRA and INDRA SPACE), two SMEs (COTESA and XENOMATIX), four cross-border SMEs (FLYSIGHT, IKH, EUROBIOS and MEWS FRANCE) and two research and technology organisations (RTOs) (RMA and BSC).

The project is organised into seven work packages (WPs), covering all the activities included in the call. PRECISE will be designed for both experts and non-expert users and will ensure its dual-use.

projects.title: Non Interferable Non Jammable Accurate Ammunition
projects.objective:
NINJA² addresses the challenges faced in GNSS-contested and denied environments, where traditional precision-guided munitions fail due to jamming and spoofing. The project will develop an affordable, modular, accurate, and intelligent ammunition system to enhance precision and resilience in all modern weapon systems, including the possibility to upgrade existing systems.
The NINJA² philosophy is focussed around creating a Modular Open System Architecture (MOSA) which will be interoperable, flexible, and scalable for all smart ammunition systems. The core idea is to enable the development of munitions with shared components and standardized interfaces, allowing for rapid integration, upgrades, and customisation across different platforms and munition types.
The NINJA² system is centred around a guidance module for artillery ammunitions, incorporating advanced technologies for navigation and guidance. The system will also incorporate AI-based target detection, recognition, and tracking, as well as scene matching technology for terminal guidance.
The project, led by Rheinmetall, will involve collaboration between various European partners and leverage existing technologies to develop this scalable and modular open architecture for guided munitions. The ultimate goal is to provide a European system that reduces the need for large quantities of ammunition while increasing precision and minimizing wear and tear on weapon systems.

projects.title: Future Air System for European Tactical Transportation 2
projects.objective:
Tactical transport aircrafts are the workhorses of battlefields, fulfilling missions like troop transport, logistics transportation, medical evacuation (MEDEVAC), and airdrop (cargo and troops alike) among other missions. The current picture of the worldwide mid-size tactical aircraft market clearly suggests there is a gap that could be filled with a new aircraft model, which has been called Future Midsize Tactical Cargo (FMTC).
Several of the most relevant aircraft makers in Europe have joint forces and knowledge in a Consortium named FASETT and its second phase named FASETT2 in order to jointly define the preliminary characteristics of FMTC, looking to combine some cutting-edge technologies with an affordable effort. This initial phase comprises technologies identification, feasibility study and risk assessment phase. 
This initial effort, under the support and funding of EU will establish the foundations of the Future Midsize Tactical Cargo aircraft, designed and manufactured in Europe and targeting to become the main midsize military transport aircraft of the western countries for the 21st century.

projects.title: European Multifunction System Concept applied to Communications, Electronic Warfare and Radar
projects.objective:
SCEPTER project seeks to combine radar, electronic warfare, and communication functions with adaptive cognitive features, enhancing situational awareness across air, land, and sea domains. It plans to create modular and interoperable systems, establishing standardized hardware and software to boost operational flexibility while minimizing electromagnetic spectrum consumption. The design will integrate advanced AESA antennas and Gallium Nitride technology to meet strict requirements for size, weight, and power in airborne platforms. Additionally, the program will tackle challenges in thermal management and signal integrity to ensure dependable performance across different environments.

The project will follow two parallel strategies: 
 - Developing essential components within Europe to secure technological autonomy and avoid dependency on foreign suppliers. This approach strengthens sovereignty by retaining control over critical subsystems for future modifications and upgrades. 
 - Adopting commercially available solutions where applicable to speed up development timelines and reduce costs. By combining internal innovation with off-the-shelf technologies, the project supports both self-sufficiency and efficiency in advancing Europe’s defense capabilities.

projects.title: European High-Performance Processor for RF Defense Applications
projects.objective:
The deployment of RF-CMOS (Radio Frequency Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor) technologies for realizing RF Systems-On-a-Chip has revolutionized many applications through its unparalleled performance, manufacturability, and cost-efficiency.  However, many of the components used in defence applications originate from outside Europe today. This dependence on external component suppliers is not really acceptable for the defense industry, where components could be withheld or delayed for strategic reasons by foreign governments. It is therefore crucial for Europe to develop an intrinsically secure, ITAR free, supply chain for building RF-based RF defence systems which delivers state of the art components and is reliable, accessible and which can be preferentially offered to European OEMs for their future requirements.
The ePERFECT project aims to formulate a strategy for the development and ownership of an advanced RF-CMOS supply chain within Europe to address these issues. To achieve the overall project aims, European end users (OEMs) with different technology requirements and products will engage with European design groups and technology suppliers, to create components for the defence electronics industry over the coming decades.
The target of ePERFECT is to develop a fine pitch European RF-CMOS supply chain that can produce high-performance RF components like transceivers and processors. These components will be validated and incorporated into next-generation digital AESA Radar, wideband communication and narrowband communication systems. Furthermore, a first review of novel 3D RF systems packaging approaches for future development is being undertaken.
The capabilities of the RF-Processor and RF-Transceivers planned for this project will be exposed to the whole European OEM community to ensure maximum awareness of their capabilities through an OEM information exchange forum. Creating a critical mass in Europe around the core elements of the supply chain.

projects.title: CARMENTA PRIMUM FUGA
projects.objective:
This Project Proposal respond to the CALL EDF-2023-DA-AIR-SPS: Self-protection systems and is the follow-on of the EDIDP 2020 project CARMENTA (Carmenta Phase 1). The overall main objective of the whole Carmenta Project is to Design, Develop, Verify, Validate, Qualify and Certify a future and innovative Self Protection System (SPS) that will increase the survivability of fixed-wing and rotary-wing, combat and non-combat aircrafts, in a hostile environment. The present proposal constitutes a second phase of the Carmenta Project and aims to design, prototyping and testing of such new generation system. The proposed project, starting from the results of the CARMENTA Phase 1 project, will proceed with the Subsystems Detailed Design categorized in three classes: High Development Item (HDI) with major technological improvements, Low Development Item (LDI) with minor technological improvements and Non-Development Item (NDI) limited to implementation of features to make them compatible with Carmenta ecosystem.

The innovative technological improvements will be verified, at subsystems and system level, by means of a Demonstrators that will be tested in a laboratory environment with an appropriate set of stimuli and monitoring. After that in order to step up to TRL 7 the design will be updated as necessary and a Prototype, for all or part of the subsystems will be realized, integrated and installed in an existing rotary wing aircraft made available by the Italian Air Force, and a Fight Test campaign will be carried. In addition, a digital model of each subsystem will be designed and developed, to be integrated in a simulation engine of overall system to support analysis and for evaluation of its behaviour when operate in different scenarios and in different configurations.

projects.title: Next Generation Military Integrated Modular Avionics
projects.objective:
In the foreseeable future, a new generation of military aerial platforms demands advanced avionics supporting innovative architectures, enhanced functionalities, and heightened performance, safety, and cyber resilience. Operating through a system-of-systems approach, these platforms need increased data sharing and processing, alongside specific requirements for development cycles and defense-related missions. 

The specific objective of this proposal is to study, design and demonstrate, within a 36-months timeframe, key components for the next generation of modular avionics, including:

- Identification of needs related to next generation military integrated modular avionics which will be used by legacy and next generation aerial platforms. 
- Study and identification of new high level architecture principles that should consider the need of multi-domain operations (from a safety perspective) and multi-level security.
- Identification, study and proof of concept of key technologies which must support possible future architecture solutions considering multiple constraints.
- Design of NG-MIMA scalable reference architecture(s) to be instantiated on different platforms.

NG-MIMA aims to revolutionize military avionics by bringing a new era of integrated modular avionics carefully tailored to address the distinctive and evolving requirements of military airborne platforms. Pioneering a novel European-scale design, the consortium emphasizes collaboration, harmonization, and integration with other initiatives (such as EICACS (EDF-2021-AIR-D-CAC)). The resulting NG-MIMA architecture and technologies will pave the foundation for future actions such as a full demonstrator or even the seed of a future European standard. Led by INDRA, the well-balance consortium comprises 26 entities from 10 EU Member States. NG-MIMA is poised to significantly contribute to European sovereignty and nurturing a robust ecosystem for military integrated modular avionics in defense.

projects.title: European Counter Unmanned Aerial Systems
projects.objective:
E-CUAS aims to the design and development of CUAS solutions for countering class I UAS, single and/or multi-UAS, including military grade UAS such as loitering munitions by integrating multiple technologies for the effective protection of critical defence infrastructure, installations and assets in fixed, mobile and deployed scenarios.
The proposed CUAS solutions will integrate a) relevant passive and active sensors technologies to detect, track, classify the threats, including the capability of detection for non-cooperative and autonomous UAS.  b) a suite of soft kill and hard kill effector technologies with an optimal selection and activation of relevant effectors with a broad set of ROE adapted to the surrounding environment and the operational scenario. c) Command and Control (C2) capabilities supporting minimum operator effort for decision making, effective sensor and effector engagement according to ROE. d) networking and communications infrastructure, cyber security and interoperability data models and protocols for standard interfaces and interoperability with relevant foreseen UAS systems (e.g. U-space) and higher air defence C2 or other units.
The E-CUAS project is in continuity and complements the project JEY-CUAS awarded in the frame of the call EDIDP2020. The E-CUAS project will study, harmonize and consolidate concepts of operations, use cases, user needs, and operational requirements; deliver harmonized system requirements and standardized CUAS open architecture and interfaces; deliver CUAS solutions system/sub-system design; deliver CUAS solutions prototypes tested and qualified in relevant defence scenarios agreed with Member States.
Four large E-CUAS demos in defence scenarios are planned in Italy, Spain, France/Belgium, Germany of CUAS for protection of critical infrastructure, forward operating base and mobile assets. A security scenario in a civilian urban context for the protection of large events in public areas is also addressed.

projects.title: European Detect & Avoid System, phase 2
projects.objective:
This project addresses the EDF-2023-DA-C4ISR-DAA topic “Detect and Avoid” (DAA), technologies that enables Remotely Piloted Aircraft Systems (RPAS) to integrate safely into non-segregated civil and military airspace, avoiding collisions with other aircraft. The DAA system observes the environment surrounding the Remotely Piloted Aircraft (RPA), detects traffic, assesses the risk of collision, informs and provides suggestions to the Remote Pilot (RP) to remain well clear and, when appropriate generates a new flight path to avoid collision. This way, DAA enables the RPA, or Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS), to operate along with other manned and unmanned aircraft, allowing a much more flexible use of RPAS/UAS than today, in order to train for, and conduct, security and defence missions in peacetime, crisis and conflict.
The European Detect and Avoid System - Part 2 project (herein abbreviated “EUDAAS2”) will complete the development of the DAA system for RPAS, designed and validated in the EDIDP 2019 Part 1 (herein abbreviated “EUDAAS1”), delivering (via EUROCAE) a full suite of required DAA standard updates supporting the provision of the ETSO baseline (by EASA), for the future system certification and product industrialization phase. 
The project’s Mission, as defined by the EDF-2023-DA-C4ISR-DAA topic scope, is to provide a fully standardised, qualified and certifiable DAA solution for UAS. The DAA solution should aim at fully integrating RPAS in the general airspace (A-G classes) without any limitations regarding operation in the airspace including in TMA.
The EUDAAS project runs over 4 years and is executed by a core the team maintained from EUDAAS1, supported by 5 contributing Member States and additional 6 nations cross-border partners. The team consist with a mix of with Defence and Aeronautics Industry groups, Aerospace Research Institutes, SMEs/MidCaps and Eurcontrol as the intergovernmental ATM organisation of Europe.

projects.title: OPTIcal MilitAry Secure communications
projects.objective:
OPTIMAS will develop a robust free-space optical (FSO) communication solution for multi-domain defence applications, showcasing one of the most challenging demonstration cases for optical communications: the link between a UAV and a satellite with an astounding Gbps data rate, analysing the future incorporation of QKD capability.
OPTIMAS will define, design, develop and test a prototype for airborne laser communication system (ALCoS) applicable to different satellite constellations (LEO, MEO and GEO) to be integrated into Air, Naval, C5ISR center and Land units. The OPTIMAS demonstrator will focus on three communication subsystems for secure and high-speed bidirectional communication: 1) A LEO satellite with accurate Pointing Acquisition and Tracking (PAT) function 2) Key enabling technologies for FSO communications 3) analysis to further include Quantum key distribution (QKD).
During its 3.5-year duration, OPTIMAS will bring into service optical communications between satellite and UAVs, demonstrate scalability and interoperability in other domains, achieve demonstrable data rates of air and satellite link in the order of Gbps, ensure beam compensation related to atmospheric conditions and divergence generated by the atmosphere, and integrate into  RPAS, easily adaptable to MALE RPAS.
OPTIMAS relies on a balanced consortium of 12 beneficiaries from 7 European countries, built on the close interaction between large companies, 5 innovative SMEs, 3 mid-caps, and 3 research organisations. With its disruptive potential, in terms of transmission speed, miniaturisation of onboarded communication solutions, piezoelectric actuation, stability/agility/pointing and QKD integration, OPTIMAS will be a game changer for ultra-secure high-bandwidth communications in warfare and battle zones.

projects.title: Advanced capabilities & Certification for Tactical UAV Systems
projects.objective:
ACTUS is built to be the most ambitious project related to the domain of tactical RPAS at European scale. 
Tactical RPAS category covers a broad range of small and medium unmanned platforms. Though they draw less attention than Long-Endurance Medium Altitude RPAS (MALE), their volume is significantly higher and their operational impact has been quite high in recent conflicts. They are engaged at the heart of the battlefield where their polyvalence and robustness makes them cost-efficient assets. 
Today they are facing new requirements to operate in higher intensity conflicts and to broaden their spectrum of operational missions. New challenges include a maturity step towards higher design level (through certification), major increase of safety of flight (with new generation flight control system), improved resilience against harsh operating conditions (GNSS-denied environment, communication jamming, icing conditions) and weapon integration using safe design standards.
Gathering the experience and expertise of proven European players in the specific TRPAS domain, ACTUS answers to these demanding requirements and is meant to constitute a significant step in the technological evolution of European tactical RPAS systems.
The ACTUS Consortium consists of 24 partners and a number of specialized subcontractors from 11 countries from South, Central and North Europe, including:• Defence primes: IDE (Coordinator),SED, TLS, SAAB, PATRIA & DIEHL; • Aircraft designers/producers: HAI, ALTUS & CYRIC • Aircraft system designers/producers: ULP, UBIQ, EMB, SED & ROB; • Software/hardware developers: GEO, REAL, AES & ISD; • RTOs: TUM, AIT & UoP • Supporting entities: DBC in charge of ethics & SGX  in charge of dissemination.
The ACTUS Consortium members have extensive background, complementarity with regards to the ACTUS scope and already established co-operations among them. The ACTUS Project is supported by 7 Ministries of Defense from 7 MSs.

EDF-2023-DA-CYBER-CSA.title: Full-Spectrum Cyber Situational Awareness for enhanced Cyberspace Operations Support

projects.title: European Cyber Situational Awareness Platform - Enhanced Cyberspace Operations
projects.objective:
ECYSAP Enhanced Cyberspace Operations (ECYSAP EYE) will conduct a new development spiral for the Cyberspace Situational Awareness and Situational Understanding (CSASU) capability initiated by EDA contracts (CySAP) and a precedent EDIDP phase (ECYSAP). This new spiral will introduce novel functionalities that responds to additional end-user requirements, Concepts of Employment (CONEMP) of additional member states, and further adaption to the continuous and evolving understanding of how operationalize cyberspace in the context of multi-domain operations. ECYSAP EYE aligns with the needs expressed in the European Union Military Vision and Strategy on Cyberspace as a Domain of Operations, being a key piece for EU CSDP Command and Control (C2) under the PESCO EUMILCOM project. Its increased level of ambition focalizes in:
•To orchestrate reasoning and projection on the perceived mission-centric cyber pictures by Mission Engineering (ME) processes, which allows exploring dependencies between cyber assets and mission context while facilitating analysts in developing customized intelligence products to assist military decision-makers. 
•To assist human beings for their more in-depth comprehension of cyberspace regardless lacking of advanced cyber skills and customized to their area of interests, operations and responsibility. ECYSAP EYE explores cyberspace activities in a continuum of conflicts that intermingle military but also civilian actors, and where cyber situations may present physical, virtual or cyber cognitive nature.
•To transition from a purely defensive posture to understand changes in cyberspace as opportunities, assessing related effects not only in terms of mission assurance (which ECYSAP EYE will also make evolve from EDIDP phase) but also opportunities. ECYSAP EYE will cover specific developments for contributing to ISTAR (Intelligence, surveillance, target acquisition, and reconnaissance). 
•To achieve a more sophisticated development of options, differentiating passive measures from proposals that confront enemy decisions in the physical, virtual and cognitive spheres. ECYSAP EYE provides tailored guidance on how to response threatening situations and/or exploit opportunities. 
•Architectural transition to a System of Systems (SoS), which benefits include easing holistic capability understanding, more effective resource sharing/pooling and adaptation to different Force/C2 structures and procedures.

ECYSAP EYE is an EU sovereign initiative motivated by all defence capability boosting directions indicated by the EU Heads of State or Government as a consequence of Russian aggression to Ukraine (Versailles, March 2022). Passively contributes to 2) step up cooperation through joint projects; 3) close shortfalls and meet EU capability objectives; 4) boost innovation, including through civil/military synergies; and 5) strengthen and develop EU defence industry, including SMEs.

projects.title: Artificial Intelligence Deployable Agent
projects.objective:
The project Artificial Intelligence Deployable Agent (AIDA) aims to develop and upgrade European defence technology in the field of cyber defence AI. The project addresses two main challenges that defence sector end-users are facing: 1) A growing attack surface due to increasing digitalisation and 2) the use of AI-based solutions for delivering cyber attacks.

As a solution, the project delivers a common European framework for developing and operating cyber defence AI agents across the entire cyber incident cycle. AIDA shall prototype AI agents for all military domains to TRL7.  The focus is on supporting operators and decision-makers within various scenarios (from preparation through response up to lessons-learned) and technologies (manned-unmanned, fixed-mobile). For successful adoption, due attention is given to AI trustworthiness, both from technical and cultural perspectives.

The solution is intended to be used in conjunction with existing or future cyber defence and security toolsets, as a way to enhance the capabilities and avoid unnecessary duplication. Nonetheless, separate AI management modules are planned within AIDA which have the potential to become a pan-European platform for cyber (defence) AI agents.

The project delivers highly needed benefits to the security and autonomy of the EU (incl., for critical infrastructure) and to the advancement of the EU’s industrial base in the strategic area of AI. In addition, the solution shall alleviate human resource availability problems and promote military mobility through securing modern autonomous vehicles and aircrafts.

EDF-2023-DA-GROUND-IFS.title: Long-range indirect fire support capabilities for precision and high efficiency strikes

projects.title: Future Indirect fiRes European Solution 2
projects.objective:
The latest conflicts underlined the need of cutting-edge rockets and 155mm artillery guided projectiles able to provide extended range, but also accuracy. Even if some capacities are still available in Europe, FIRES 2 project will place Europe as a future key player in the world.
FIRES 2, as a continuity of the EDIDP FIRES project, proposes an unprecedented approach combining two initially different types of ammunition (155mm artillery shell and rockets) into a single project that stimulates complementary and commonalities at sub-system and component levels. 
The project will prepare a roadmap to develop this future ammunition family with longer range and higher accuracy within the timeframe 2030, offering an artillery projectile able to cover 60km at least, offering a 10m class CEP and a rocket able to cover 150 km range minimum and with a 10m class CEP. For this purpose, FIRES 2 will use FIRES’ output to save time on the research phase, push forward feasibility studies and perform a comprehensive design of the most innovative concepts for the following subsystems: propulsion, seeker, airframe, GNC, communications command and control, payload, computer vision, computational unit and power supply. Furthermore, FIRES 2 intends to carry forward with subsystem prototyping and de-risking tests on key technologies identified as paramount to reach extended range and accuracy: propulsion, seeker, GNC, and fuze (payload). 
The critical subsystems de-risked in FIRES 2 will reach qualification in a subsequent “FIRES 3” project (2028-2030). The European supply chain is expected to achieve an EU critical components production capability to setup the industrialization phase for 2032. This will reinforce EU’s independence and competitiveness addressing concurrent markets by creating cost-effective in-house alternatives based on commonalities and interoperability between existing and future technology. This 36-months cross-border project will gather 21 Entities from 13 European Countries, more than half of which were already part of the FIRES project.

projects.title: Main ARmoured Tank of Europe
projects.objective: The MARTE project addresses the need for greater European defence autonomy and superiority by addressing the crucial area of the Main Battle Tank (MBT) where many EU Member States (MS) operate outdated MBT models. Key objectives of MARTE are to: (1) Study and design of a new European MBT platform as well consideration of technology upgrades for current MBTs altogether addressing all future threats and needs, operating in diverse environments, offering superior protection, detection, and firepower; (2) Increase the competitive advantage of European companies in the worldwide MBT industry, fostering economic growth and high-tech job creation; (3) Reinforce EU Defence Technological and Industrial Base autonomy, reduce dependence on third-country innovations focusing on European technologies ; (4) Foster cross-border cooperation, involving 15 SMEs and 4 Mid-Caps; (5) Enhance cost-effectiveness and lifecycle efficiency compared to existing MBT solutions; (6) Further contribute to the integration of the European defence market with the involvement of 49 entities and support from 11 MS and Norway. MARTE will be implemented through six work packages. The project will begin with CONOPS and System requirements, followed by technology studies and an evaluation. System and subsystem specifications will be performed and completed with the system and subsystem design description. System Architecture will be designed, resulting in the Preliminary Design Review as the final output of the project. MARTE will be efficiently managed thanks to a dedicated company (MARTE ARGE), a well-defined methodology, identified milestones and KPIs. The consortium aims to achieve project objectives within 24 months, with an EDF support of 20M€. MARTE's innovative approach and large-scale European collaboration will pave the way to the market introduction of the new MBT by 2035.

projects.title: Technologies for existing and Future MBTs
projects.objective: The use of the Main Battle Tank (MBT) in military operations represents one of the most important innovations in modern warfare. Undoubtedly, the MBT has been key to battle superiority in particular when using technological advantages in novel and disruptive ways. However, for the last decades the MBT has mainly seen small step enhancements while lethality and multitude of threats from low to high tech have increased dramatically and the potential battlespace environment has widened to include expeditionary and closer to home operations. In order for the MBT to effectively reach in the future the desired military objectives, the role, characteristics and tactical utilisation of the MBT within operations have to be modernised, and keep pace with battlespace evolution and digitisation, using technologies efficiently and effectively to provide a coherent Land Systems Battle force. To achieve this objective, FMBTech aims to develop a modular design for agile, adaptive, intelligent and cooperative current/new/future MBT systems, able to deliver cutting edge Through Life Capabilities (TLC). These Capabilities will be based on a variety of state-of-the-art Technology Bricks that will provide seamless functional services across agile integrated modular open scalable architectures for the MBT as the centre of a Digitised Capabilities Envelope, giving enhanced ability to interface with unmanned systems and increased crew efficiency thanks to AI. This approach lends itself to commonality and interchangeability of equipment across the battle force offering coherent capabilities, and is supported by a solid Model Based Systems Engineering process Preliminary Design. Case Studies for current/new/future MBTs with simulations advocated by Business Cases will ensure feasibility of a coherent roadmap of sustainable development for EU defence TLC MBTs, as well as early de-risking the Next Generation of MBTs and potential early exploitation of disruptive technologies in current MBTs.

projects.title: Maintenance, Joining, and Repair innovation in multidomain defence
projects.objective: MaJoR is a development project to enable and advance joining, maintenance and repair technologies from Europe's R&T, industry base and SMEs and achieve up to TRL6 through demonstrator activities in three defence domains – air, sea and land. The project addresses the need to expand the EU defence industry's capability and secure European autonomy through cross-border and multi-domain collaboration. Each domain will develop a platform representing the test environment for various technologies developed within MaJoR. For the air domain, a fuselage of a UAV, also known as a Remote Carrier, will be prototyped, the sea domain platform comprises a lightweight, integrated ship mast structure, and lastly, the land platform will be a hybrid truck cabin made of fibre reinforced plastic (FRP). In total, seven technology streams are defined: Bonding and welding system development for composite and multi-materials, Material modelling and simulation/design and sizing method, Integrated structural design incl. integrated functions, Assembly process technology addressing manufacturing scalability, SHM - Inspection and monitoring, Multi-material repairs incl. composites, and Metallic repairs. Furthermore, MaJoR shall empower Europe's SMEs and start-ups with the FSTP programme by providing them financial support (max. €60k per candidate, ≤60 in total) and technical/entrepreneurship mentoring to bring their technologies to the European defence market. MaJoR consortium comprises 35 beneficiaries and one affiliated partner, covering ten member states, and a total grant of €30 million. Through the development and application of the technologies in MaJoR, the project aims to bring down assembly cost by 40%, save 20% weight, increase production speed by 30%, accelerate the design process by 30%, increase 10% more space for systems, reduce the radar cross section (RCS) signature and increase vehicle availability, thereby strengthening the mission-readiness of EU defence.

projects.title: Enhancing cross-border CBRN operational readiness and effectiveness through an overarching System of Systems approach
projects.objective:
The main idea of CBRN SoS (System of Systems) is to design an overarching information system that can integrate different existing national CBRN defence information systems and components in a modular and flexible way. It can be permanently stationed in a specific configuration of core components ready for on demand deployment. Additional elements from different Nations can be added dynamically depending on specific needs of the operation. 
CBRN SoS addresses Detection, Identification and Monitoring (DIM), Knowledge Management (KM), Physical Protection (PP), and Hazard Management (HM). First an EU CBRN solution inventory study for these components will be conducted. These results will be analysed to identify capability improvements in existing information systems and map out synergies between Member States to define potential entry points for the federated CBRN SoS. Subsequently, the detailed system architecture design will be derived, and key representative parts prototyped to validate the system through demonstrations.
The main advantages of CBRN SoS result from the ability for multiple entities from different Member States to collaborate which enhances the overall situational awareness and leads to a faster and more efficient CBRN threat response. 
Using ATP 45 elements as key pillars for the distributed CBRN SoS software architecture, is an attempt to bring a well-established and known system structure into the fragmented landscape of isolated CBRN solutions. In this way different Nations can relate and map own structures to CBRN SoS more easily, which eventually improves the recognizability and identification of end-users with CBRN SoS. Rather than developing a completely new software solution from scratch, CBRN SoS uses the more efficient and more realistic approach to focus on interfaces to existing solutions and modules, as Member States want to continue using their own solutions and providers but still be able to cooperate in a federated way.

projects.title: EUROPEAN INTERACTIVE SENSOR-BASED DYNAMIC DEFENCE NETWORK
projects.objective:
Today’s EU Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD) sensor centric weapons systems need to be improved to increase their resilience and reactivity against the emergence of new threats and the repercussions that they have. These new threats are stealthier, faster, greater in number, and more agile by their manoeuvrability. The combination of those threats used in synchronized strikes often confuse and saturate current IAMD weapon systems.
The NIAG Working group 260 (NATO Industry Advisory Industry Group) recommended that IAMD suppliers should move away from a sensor centric to a network centric approach through the creation of a network of FCCs and sensors referred to as a ‘sensor grid’ which would allow real time sharing of data and services between sensors in a highly dynamic and efficient manner. 
In this way improvements in operational performance are achieved through a combination of fast data rate dynamic sensor tasking, data fusion, and the effective distribution of services in real-time (below a second) over the dynamic decision-making sensor grid. It obtains the benefit of the multiple sensors coverage, resources and data combined  while accelerating the exchange of information up and down the control and command chain.
The overriding ambition of the project is to define the EISNET architecture framework tool (EAFT) that will consist in a set of common concepts, rules and functions that will permit the scalability of heterogenous weapon systems, sensors and FCC with different architectures or configurations cooperating in a network centric solution. To achieve this, FCC and sensor suppliers will have to implement progressively and seamlessly these concepts, rules and functions in their systems to be compliant with the EAFT. Heterogeneity of technologies often considered as a weakness of the European defence market will become a benefit through the combining of these different sensors, weapons and FCC and use them as a unified system while preserving their autonomy and sovereignty in usage and on key technologies. This is a new tactical advantage as the attacking threats are faced with uncertainty while the diversity of systems with real-time dynamic tactics management contributes to an ever better resilience.

projects.title: European Military Integrated Space Situational Awareness and Recognition capabilitY
projects.objective:
EMISSARY proposal gives a comprehensive response to the growing dependency and threats facing space infrastructure, which are vital for global positioning, navigation, intelligence, and secure communications in military operations. 
The EU Council Conclusions on the EU Space Strategy for Security and Defence underscore the importance of Space Domain Awareness (SDA) for national sovereignty and encourage sharing SDA information among Member States. EMISSARY aligns with these conclusions, aiming to support the establishment of a complete and independent European military SSA value-chain. 

EMISSARY’s value proposition focuses on four interconnected beacons: sovereignty in detection, tracking, and characterization of space objects; shared European recognized space picture to sMS based on enhanced SSA data; integration into the international landscape; and establishing a robust European data security policy. It emphasizes the development of a network of advanced sensors and a military SSA Command and Control (C2) software to process and circulate data, ensuring national and European sovereignty while fostering EDTIB collaboration.

The project involves government agencies, industries, SME and midcaps, scientific communities and RTOs, and integrating international initiatives and innovative technologies. It encompasses 51 partners from 13 Member States, 12 of which has signed the Letter of Intent, ensuring continuity and expansion of expertise from previous projects like INTEGRAL and SAURON. EMISSARY aims to achieve a prototyping, demonstration and qualification stage for a large set of the technologies developed, reflecting a commitment to advancing European military SSA capabilities, ensuring security and sustainability in space, and enhancing Europe's strategic autonomy and international competitiveness in the space domain.

projects.title: SEabed and Anti-submarine warfare Capability through Unmanned featuRe for Europe
projects.objective:
European Naval Forces are going to face new threats such as proliferation of submarines, unmanned systems, heavy/light/very light weight torpedoes, hyper-fast torpedoes. It introduces new operational theatres, requires new capabilities and new concepts of operation. Anti-submarine warfare and Seabed warfare are more than ever two topics of utmost importance for the EU and its security
European navies can no longer deal with this evolution of threats without new technology and systems specifically designed for them. However, they need to experiment at sea in order to refine their operational concepts and get confidence in this new technology before launching long and expensive acquisition programs. This is exactly the overall purpose of SEACURE EDF project to develop, demonstrate at sea and evaluate an integrated system of unmanned platforms to successfully perform joint ASW and SBW operations to protect critical maritime infrastructure, ready to join conventional Task Forces. 

The key innovation of this project is to base the development on an open and agile architecture, interoperable using NATO standards, modular and scalable. The solution will be composed of a command, control and communication system, a set of maritime unmanned systems (aerial, surface and underwater), along with their dedicated payloads and effectors. The aim will be to reach at least a maturity level equivalent to technology readiness level 6. One of the main challenges being to bring the navigation autonomy and mission autonomy at this level and reduce the number of operators. 

In the future the system developed in SEACURE could be proposed in various configurations and extended to other missions (ISR, Anti surface warfare, Mine Warfare). Its ambition is to enable EU navies to deal with numerous simultaneous missions in larger areas of operations. It will leverage from the synergies of all the consortium members to achieve this overarching goal.

projects.title: European Extended Mine Countermeasures (E=MCM)
projects.objective:
The maritime mine threat is a reality for both the civil and military shipping since more than a century and the consequences can be ranged from blocking access to harbours, coastal waters and specific maritime areas to hindering drastically maritime trade and the freedom of manoeuvre of naval ships. In EU, the sea mine threat has been a constant – although changing – reality for decades and it is crucial that the EU Allies strengthen their capacities to counter effectively mine threats appearing in broad maritime spectrum zones that encompass surf zone to deep water including chokepoints, SLOCs delivering enabling essential trade such as communications, and energies to EU citizens, companies, governments. In that context, the European Extended Mine Countermeasures (E=MCM) project aims to close development gaps for capabilities of the Toolbox 2.0 by delivering better, faster, safer and more resilient MCM operations by designing, prototyping and demonstrating a single consistent system of systems (SoS) composed of: (i) advanced evolving & scalable unmanned autonomous toolboxes; (ii) intelligent platforms and decision support tools to achieve significant operational gains in terms of quality, performance and efficiency; (iii) incremental capabilities to address and counter, with increased effectiveness, the current and future mine threat ((drifting, tethered, and buried mines).

The consortium of E=MCM project is composed of 21 beneficiaries from 11 EU nations with 9 large companies, 2 Midcaps, 6 SMEs and 4 RTOs and the work is structured into 12 WPs: 3 WPs are related to studies, 4 WPs are related to Design activities, 4 WPs to Prototyping activities and 1 WP related to Testing activities.  
7 sea trials will be conducted across the overall EU (BE, NL, FR, DE, ES) and the total eligible cost of the E=MCM project is 69,87M€ with 45M€ from EU grant and 23,87M€ from 10 supporting EU Member States (BE, DE, EL, ES, FR, LT, LV, NL, PT, SE)

projects.title: ARCHitectures based on unconventional accelerators for dependable/energY efficienT AI Systems
projects.objective:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) can power autonomous vehicles, provide strategic advantages through large-scale data analytics, and enable intelligence gathering and surveillance through advanced computer vision, opening a wide range of defence applications.

Conventional Von Neumann architectures, despite their flexibility, are inefficient for AI workloads due to data duplication and data movement bottlenecks, which severely limit the efficiency in the rapid processing of large amount of information and streaming data needed by AI algorithms.

In edge computing devices, crucial for defense applications, this inefficiency is compounded by energy limitations.

To overcome these challenges, specialised hardware and programming paradigm shift are needed to accelerate AI workloads. With the end of Moore’s law and Dennard scaling, simply scaling up existing architectures is no longer viable. Novel architectures are needed to improve performance per Watt and bypass the efficiency limits imposed by the Von Neumann bottleneck.

ARCHYTAS aims to investigate unconventional AI accelerators that take advantage of novel technologies: optoelectronic-based accelerators, volatile and non-volatile processing-in-memory, and neuromorphic devices. These technologies promise to mitigate the Von Neumann bottleneck by integrating processing and memory. ARCHYTAS also explores the integration of CMOS-based systems with analogue accelerators, as well as new programming models to improve the programmability, performance portability, and productivity of these emerging parallel systems through a hardware-AI co-design approach. The technological ambition of ARCHYTAS is to bridge the gaps in multi-modal sensing integration and AI processing, providing solutions that fit the non-functional requirements of future autonomous vehicles for defense applications. 

The ARCHYTAS AI accelerators will be validated within the context of defense AI use cases in land, aerial, maritime, and space settings.
